facebook.caption:
This place fixes something you didn't know was broken.

Dinh Cậu is a small shrine to the sea goddess, sitting right at the edge of the harbor. Blue-and-white lighthouse behind it. Fishing boats in front. Locals come here at sunrise and sunset to pray, light incense, and just breathe.

It costs nothing to visit. It takes 20 minutes. It feels completely different from the rest of Phu Quoc.

Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.
facebook.video_caption: Incense smoke drifting over the harbor at golden hour. Fishing boats bobbing. A lighthouse you almost walk past. Dinh Cậu hits different when the crowds clear. Go before 7am or right at sunset.

instagram.caption:
Phu Quoc that doesn't feel touristy. At all.

Dinh Cậu. Sea goddess shrine. Harbor views. Free to visit. Go at sunrise.

Save this.
instagram.video_caption: Incense at the harbor. Lighthouse. Fishing boats. Dinh Cậu at sunrise hits different. 4.5 stars, 3,497 reviews.

tiktok.caption: The most peaceful 20 mins in Phu Quoc. Dinh Cậu at sunrise. Free. Go. #PhuQuoc #VietnamTravel #PhuQuocTips

scenes: Slow pan across harbor at dawn
Close-up: incense smoke rising

items.placeName: Dinh Bà Thủy Long Thánh Mẫu

facebook.caption:
POV: you took a wrong turn on Võ Thị Sáu and found this.

Dinh Bà Thủy Long Thánh Mẫu is one of those places most tourists walk straight past. A working temple in the middle of Khu 1, 4.5 stars from 282 reviews, and almost nobody from the resort crowd knows it exists.

The incense, the colour, the quiet. Completely different side of Phu Quoc.

Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.
facebook.video_caption: Smoke rising, offerings lined up, locals praying mid-morning. Dinh Bà Thủy Long Thánh Mẫu on Võ Thị Sáu is the real Phu Quoc most visitors never see. 4.5 stars. Zero tourist crowds. Tag your travel buddy.

facebook.caption: 13,636 reviews. 4.5 stars. Grand World Phu Quoc keeps delivering. This massive entertainment complex at Bãi Dài is where the island turns up the volume. Canal streets, live performances, night markets, restaurants, and lights that go hard after dark. One ticket in, zero excuses for a boring night. Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.
facebook.video_caption: Walking through Grand World Phu Quoc at night and the place does not stop. Canal boats, fire shows, street food, crowds that actually have energy. 13,636 people reviewed this for a reason.
